  • Method Details

    • createOptionOrder

      public OptionOrderServiceCreateOptionOrderRequestBuilder createOptionOrder()
      Create Option Order

      Creates a new option order.

      Currently only single-leg option orders are supported, but the data structures will support multi-leg orders in the future. The single-leg constraint will be repeated in this documentation, but validation rules related to the initial (future) multi-leg support are also documented.

      Upon successful submission, if the request is a duplicate, returns the existing order in its current state in the system. If the request is not a duplicate, returns the summary of the newly submitted order.

      Returns:
      The call builder

    • createOptionOrder

      public OptionOrderServiceCreateOptionOrderResponse createOptionOrder(String accountId, OptionOrderCreate optionOrderCreate) throws Exception
      Create Option Order

      Creates a new option order.

      Currently only single-leg option orders are supported, but the data structures will support multi-leg orders in the future. The single-leg constraint will be repeated in this documentation, but validation rules related to the initial (future) multi-leg support are also documented.

      Upon successful submission, if the request is a duplicate, returns the existing order in its current state in the system. If the request is not a duplicate, returns the summary of the newly submitted order.

      Parameters:
      accountId - The account id.
      optionOrderCreate - The message describing an option order
      Returns:
      The response from the API call
      Throws:
      Exception - if the API call fails

    • createOptionOrder

      public OptionOrderServiceCreateOptionOrderResponse createOptionOrder(String accountId, OptionOrderCreate optionOrderCreate, Optional<Options> options) throws Exception
      Create Option Order

      Creates a new option order.

      Currently only single-leg option orders are supported, but the data structures will support multi-leg orders in the future. The single-leg constraint will be repeated in this documentation, but validation rules related to the initial (future) multi-leg support are also documented.

      Upon successful submission, if the request is a duplicate, returns the existing order in its current state in the system. If the request is not a duplicate, returns the summary of the newly submitted order.

      Parameters:
      accountId - The account id.
      optionOrderCreate - The message describing an option order
      options - additional options
      Returns:
      The response from the API call
      Throws:
      Exception - if the API call fails

    • getOptionOrder

      public OptionOrderServiceGetOptionOrderRequestBuilder getOptionOrder()
      Get Option Order

      Gets an option order by option order ID.

      Upon successful submission, returns the details of the queried order.

      Returns:
      The call builder

    • getOptionOrder

      public OptionOrderServiceGetOptionOrderResponse getOptionOrder(String accountId, String optionOrderId) throws Exception
      Get Option Order

      Gets an option order by option order ID.

      Upon successful submission, returns the details of the queried order.

      Parameters:
      accountId - The account id.
      optionOrderId - The optionOrder id.
      Returns:
      The response from the API call
      Throws:
      Exception - if the API call fails

    • getOptionOrder

      public OptionOrderServiceGetOptionOrderResponse getOptionOrder(String accountId, String optionOrderId, Optional<Options> options) throws Exception
      Get Option Order

      Gets an option order by option order ID.

      Upon successful submission, returns the details of the queried order.

      Parameters:
      accountId - The account id.
      optionOrderId - The optionOrder id.
      options - additional options
      Returns:
      The response from the API call
      Throws:
      Exception - if the API call fails

    • cancelOptionOrder

      public OptionOrderServiceCancelOptionOrderRequestBuilder cancelOptionOrder()
      Cancel Option Order

      Submits an order cancellation request by option order ID. Confirmation of order cancellation requests are provided through asynchronous events.

      Upon successful submission, returns the details of the order pending cancellation.

      Returns:
      The call builder

    • cancelOptionOrder

      public OptionOrderServiceCancelOptionOrderResponse cancelOptionOrder(String accountId, String optionOrderId, CancelOptionOrderRequestCreate cancelOptionOrderRequestCreate) throws Exception
      Cancel Option Order

      Submits an order cancellation request by option order ID. Confirmation of order cancellation requests are provided through asynchronous events.

      Upon successful submission, returns the details of the order pending cancellation.

      Parameters:
      accountId - The account id.
      optionOrderId - The optionOrder id.
      cancelOptionOrderRequestCreate - The message to request cancellation of an existing option order
      Returns:
      The response from the API call
      Throws:
      Exception - if the API call fails

    • cancelOptionOrder

      public OptionOrderServiceCancelOptionOrderResponse cancelOptionOrder(String accountId, String optionOrderId, CancelOptionOrderRequestCreate cancelOptionOrderRequestCreate, Optional<Options> options) throws Exception
      Cancel Option Order

      Submits an order cancellation request by option order ID. Confirmation of order cancellation requests are provided through asynchronous events.

      Upon successful submission, returns the details of the order pending cancellation.

      Parameters:
      accountId - The account id.
      optionOrderId - The optionOrder id.
      cancelOptionOrderRequestCreate - The message to request cancellation of an existing option order
      options - additional options
      Returns:
      The response from the API call
      Throws:
      Exception - if the API call fails
